MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 15.1 AND INTERROGATORY NO. 2. THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE TO BE PROVIDED WITHIN 21 DAYS. AS TO THE RFPDS, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WRITTEN RESPONSES ARE CODE COMPLIANT. TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY RESPONSIVE, NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS HAVE NOT BEEN PRODUCED, DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PRODUCE THEM WITHIN 21 DAYS. ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD ON PRIVILEGE MUST BE PLACED ON A LOG. AS TO THE 911 TAPE, DEFENDANT CCSF SHALL PRODUCE THE TAPE SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER ABOUT THE TERMS OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. IF AN AGREEMENT CANNOT BE REACHED, DEFENDANT SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AND PLAINTIFF MAY FILE ANY OBJECTIONS. SANCTIONS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARCELA MARTINEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Complete Verified Discovery Responses Of Defendants Another Planet Entertainment LLC, CCSF And Tida To Set 1 Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production

Motion granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 and Interrogatory No. 2. The supplemental responses are to be provided within 21 days. As to the RFPDs, the Court finds that the written responses are code compliant. To the extent that any responsive, non-privileged documents have not been produced, Defendants are ordered to produce them within 21 days. Any documents withheld on privilege must be placed on a log. As to the 911 tape, Defendant CCSF shall produce the tape subject to a protective order. The parties are directed to meet and confer about the terms of the protective order. If an agreement cannot be reached, Defendant shall submit a proposed order to the Court and Plaintiff may file any objections. Sanctions denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: David McDonald; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
